title: Fail loudly rather than drop data
description: Route unparseable or rejected records to a dead-letter path and surface counts, instead of silently filtering them out.
body: Use when handling malformed or unexpected records. Do not wrap parsing in a catch-all that discards bad rows — the pipeline then 'succeeds' while quietly losing data nobody notices for weeks. Actions: send rejected records to a dead-letter/quarantine table with the error and offset, emit reject counts as metrics, and alert when the rate exceeds a threshold. Validation signals include reconciled input vs output+rejected counts and a non-empty dead-letter trail for failures. Risk boundary: silent row loss is the hardest data bug to detect after the fact; treat any catch-and-drop in a load path as a defect needing a dead-letter route.
